Wikipedia: Clovis Regional Airport
Wikipedia: Clovis Regional Airport — Clovis-Santa Fe-Denver route operated 2012-2014

Cavalcade of Wings: Carlsbad history — Permian served Hobbs/Midland/Odessa route 1979-1981

Wikipedia: Canyonlands Regional Airport — PHX flight stopped at Page
Wikipedia: Canyonlands Regional Airport — Great Lakes added VEL nonstop before 2014 closure

Wikipedia: Canyonlands Regional Airport — Frontier 580s served ABQ, ELP, PHX, SLC, TUS via stops

note: Frontier Convair 580s served full western network from Moab 1965-1980; Great Lakes ended Jan 2014; SkyWest SLC service ended April 2015

Wikipedia: Elko Regional Airport — flights to Reno ended 2005
Wikipedia: Elko Regional Airport — Scenic served EKO-ELY-LAS through late 1990s
Wikipedia: Elko Regional Airport — Casino Express weekend 737/MD-80 charters 1994-1995

note: United served Elko as part of transcontinental route 1931-1982; current SkyWest SLC service only remaining route

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Roswell International Air Center — TI DC-9 LAX-ABQ-ROW-MAF-DAL-HOU ended Oct 1 1979
Wikipedia: Roswell International Air Center — TI ended service Oct 1 1979
Wikipedia: Roswell International Air Center — commuter era served ELP, AMA, MAF post-1979

detail: Texas International's DC-9 jet service ended October 1 1979, stripping Roswell of nonstop jet access to LA and Houston; today served only by AA to DFW (EAS) and United to DEN launched Feb 2026.
source: Wikipedia: Roswell International Air Center; Simple Flying: AA Roswell cancellation suspended

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Las Cruces International Airport — Mesa/Westward served ABQ 1985-2005
Wikipedia: Las Cruces International Airport — Zia served ELP occasionally 1974-1980
Wikipedia: Las Cruces International Airport — Zia served SAF 1974-1980

detail: Las Cruces lost all scheduled service in July 2005 and entered an 18-year hiatus before Advanced Air restored subsidized ABQ flights in January 2023 under New Mexico's RASE program.
source: Wikipedia: Las Cruces International Airport; Advanced Air LRU page

lostDestinations.source: Cavalcade of Wings: Gallup Municipal Airport — America West Express PHX ended 1999
Cavalcade of Wings: Gallup Municipal Airport — Mesa EAS ABQ ended Nov 16 2002

detail: Gallup lost EAS eligibility November 16 2002 due to low passenger traffic; went 14 years without service before Advanced Air launched PHX flights August 1 2022 via New Mexico RASE grant of $3.5M.
source: Cavalcade of Wings: Gallup Municipal Airport; Advanced Air GUP page

lostDestinations.source: Cavalcade of Wings: Silver City-Grant County Airport — Frontier ended SVC service late 1979
Cavalcade of Wings: Silver City-Grant County Airport — Frontier TUS/PHX route dropped 1974
Wikipedia: Las Cruces International Airport / Cavalcade of Wings — Aztec served LCR-ELP-SVC 1966-67

detail: Frontier ended mainline jet service in 1979; airport has operated continuously under EAS since 1980, currently served by Advanced Air to ABQ and PHX under subsidy.
source: Cavalcade of Wings: Silver City-Grant County Airport; Advanced Air SVC page

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Winslow–Lindbergh Regional Airport — Golden Pacific served PHX 1985-1987
Wikipedia: Winslow–Lindbergh Regional Airport — commuter ABQ service ended 1987
Wikipedia: Winslow–Lindbergh Regional Airport — Frontier served DEN-PHX/ABQ-PHX route through Winslow until 1974
Wikipedia: Winslow–Lindbergh Regional Airport — Desert Pacific flew direct to LAX 1980-1981

detail: Airport has had no scheduled airline service since Golden Pacific ceased in 1987; Frontier's mainline DC-3/Convair 580 service ended 1974 after which only small commuters operated briefly.
source: Wikipedia: Winslow–Lindbergh Regional Airport; Old Trails Museum aviation history panel

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Sierra Vista Municipal Airport — Great Lakes ended Feb 28 2007
Wikipedia: Sierra Vista Municipal Airport — Mesa/AWE served TUS and PHX 1989-2002

detail: Great Lakes Airlines ended the last scheduled commercial service February 28 2007; the airport had continuous scheduled service for over 40 years from the 1960s but has had none since, operating as a joint-use civil-military field.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Lake Havasu City Airport — Mesa/USAirways Express ended May 5 2007
Wikipedia: Lake Havasu City Airport — Mesa served PHX and LAS until May 5 2007

detail: Mesa Airlines operating as US Airways Express (previously America West Express) ended all service May 5 2007; Lake Havasu City had 6,082 enplanements in 2006 but the airport has had no commercial service since.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Show Low Regional Airport — Arizona Pacific/Scenic/Great Lakes/Sunrise served PHX 1994-2000
Wikipedia: Show Low Regional Airport — Great Lakes served PHX 1996-1997

detail: Show Low has been entirely EAS-dependent since 2000; Southern Airways Express currently receives $1,672,000/year federal subsidy; lost all non-subsidized service after Sunrise Airlines ceased in 2000.
source: Wikipedia: Show Low Regional Airport; AZ DOT airport profile

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia/Grokipedia: Page Municipal Airport — Great Lakes served PHX (via GCN) until Mar 2018 shutdown
Grokipedia: Page Municipal Airport — Bonanza/Air West/Hughes served SLC via CDC 1960s-1982
Grokipedia: Page Municipal Airport — Hughes served LAS via GCN from early 1970s

detail: Great Lakes Airlines sudden shutdown in March 2018 caused enplanements to plummet from 6,830 peak (2012) to 736; Contour Airlines resumed EAS service fall 2018.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Vernal Regional Airport — Great Lakes flew BHC 1900Ds to DEN from Jan 2008, ceased DEN then all service
Wikipedia: Vernal Regional Airport — Air Midwest nonstop SLC July 2006; Great Lakes replaced Jan 2008
Wikipedia: Vernal Regional Airport — original Frontier DC-3s flew nonstop SLC and RKS, left by 1982

detail: Frontier served Vernal for over 30 years (1950-1982) with DC-3 service to SLC and Rock Springs; airport now fully EAS-dependent with Contour Airlines subsidized service.

lostDestinations.source: Grokipedia: Page Municipal Airport / Cedar City Regional Airport Wikipedia — Hughes/Bonanza served SLC-CDC-PGA-PHX route
DOT Order 2024-12-6: SkyWest receives $2,564,371/yr subsidy for 12 weekly RT CDC-SLC on CRJ-200
Wikipedia: Cedar City Regional Airport — Hughes Airwest served LAS via Page route early 1970s

detail: SkyWest Airlines receives $2,564,371 annual EAS subsidy (DOT Order 2024-12-6) for 12 weekly round trips to Salt Lake City on CRJ-200; airport lost unsubsidized jet service and multiple destinations by the 1980s.
source: DOT EAS Order 2024-12-6 (regulations.gov); Wikipedia: Cedar City Regional Airport

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Logan-Cache Airport — Key Airlines served SLC 1964-1976
Wikipedia: Logan-Cache Airport — Western/West Coast Airlines served SLC 1946-1964

detail: Western Airlines began service in 1946 and West Coast Airlines continued until 1964; Key Airlines served until 1976, after which Logan-Cache Airport lost all scheduled passenger service and now handles only cargo and general aviation.
source: Wikipedia: Logan-Cache Airport; cachecounty.gov airport page

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Magic Valley Regional Airport — Hughes Airwest/Republic served LAX via DC-9 until Republic exit
Wikipedia: Magic Valley Regional Airport — Hughes Airwest flew SFO-TWF with DC-9 jet service
Wikipedia: Magic Valley Regional Airport — Hughes Airwest/Republic served SEA from TWF
Idaho State Journal: Twin Falls airport loses Denver flights — United Express DEN discontinued 2021

detail: Hughes Airwest operated a hub at Twin Falls in 1976 with eight daily DC-9 flights to 10+ cities; after Republic's exit in 1986 jet service collapsed; United Express DEN service lost in 2021 COVID aftermath.
source: Wikipedia: Magic Valley Regional Airport; Idaho State Journal article on Denver loss

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Lewiston–Nez Perce County Airport — Republic Aug 1982 timetable: nonstop BOI continuing to TWF-SLC-BUR
Wikipedia / bigcountrynewsconnection.com: Horizon served LWS Mar 1983-Aug 2018
Wikipedia: Lewiston–Nez Perce County Airport — Republic nonstop BOI-LWS on DC-9 until Sep 1982
bigcountrynewsconnection.com: Lewiston-Nez Perce County Airport Ends Denver Air Service article Dec 2024

detail: Horizon Air served Lewiston from 1983 to 2018 (35 years) first as independent then as Alaska Airlines code-share; Denver service ended December 2024 leaving only SLC and SEA connections.
source: Wikipedia: Lewiston–Nez Perce County Airport; Big Country News Connection Dec 2024
